summary:
There is no direct evidence linking general practitioner hospitals to reduced utilization of general hospital beds, as the concept is ambiguously defined and lacks supporting studies. While primary care can prevent certain hospitalizations, it does not translate to proven effects on bed usage when applied through so-called “general practitioner hospitals.”  

Final Answer: \boxed{C}

Final Answer: \boxed{C}. Answer choice: C
